title: Documentation Update Procedure
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Defines the controlled process for converting verified work into permanent repository and documentation records during WS086 reconciliation.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This procedure defines how completed Two Marshalls Studios work is converted into permanent, structured documentation. Its purpose is to prevent important knowledge from remaining only in chat history, temporary notes, screenshots, local files, or a single workstation.

sections.title: Core Rule
paragraphs: Each completed work session should be added to the structured documentation records.

Documentation is part of the work itself. A session is not fully closed until its important results, reasoning, changes, tests, decisions, bugs, fixes, lessons, and next actions have been captured where they belong.

sections.title: Source-of-Truth Rule
paragraphs: TMS-OS and StudioDB govern structured records.
The Studio Portal is the governed presentation layer.
Repository JSON documents are the implementation record for permanent portal documentation.
Google Headquarters may preserve human-readable documentation where applicable.
Chat and temporary artifacts provide evidence but are not permanent sources of truth.
items: Structured records remain governed by TMS-OS / StudioDB.
Human-readable permanent documents are published to the approved Google Headquarters structure.
GitHub preserves source code and project history.
Chat may assist with recovery and documentation generation, but important knowledge must not exist only in chat.

sections.title: When This Procedure Must Be Used
items: At the close of every controlled work session.
After a bug is fixed and verified.
After a milestone or checkpoint is completed.
After an approved decision, standard, or procedure is established.
After architecture changes or important technical lessons.
When an enhancement idea is identified but deferred.
When historical recovery produces verified information that must be preserved.

sections.title: Required Documentation Review
paragraphs: Before closing a session, review the work and determine whether it produced any of the following:
items: Work-session record
Decision
Standard
Procedure
Bug and fix
Lesson learned
Technical debt
Enhancement idea
Milestone or checkpoint
Architecture change
Recommendation
Risk
Release or migration record
Current-state change
Documentation update

sections.title: Step 1 — Confirm the Work Is Verified
paragraphs: Do not document an untested result as complete. Confirm the final state, test result, and any remaining limitations.

sections.title: Step 2 — Identify Documentation-Worthy Knowledge
paragraphs: Review what changed, why it changed, what failed, what was fixed, what was learned, and what must happen next.

sections.title: Step 3 — Classify Each Record
paragraphs: Place each item in the correct structured record type. Do not force unrelated knowledge into one general note.

sections.title: Step 4 — Preserve the Reasoning
paragraphs: Record why a decision or change was made, not only the final outcome.

sections.title: Step 5 — Update Structured Records
paragraphs: Add or update the appropriate TMS-OS / StudioDB records using the approved schema and identifiers.

sections.title: Step 6 — Validate the Records
paragraphs: Confirm required fields, IDs, relationships, and record types are structurally valid. Check for invalid records and duplicate IDs.

sections.title: Step 7 — Update Human-Readable Documentation
paragraphs: Update the structured permanent repository document and any required human-readable documentation after verification.

sections.title: Step 8 — Verify Location and Naming
paragraphs: Confirm the document is in the correct Studio Headquarters folder and uses the approved document name.

sections.title: Step 9 — Remove Temporary Files
paragraphs: After a Word upload has been converted and the native Google Doc verified, remove the temporary .docx file.

sections.title: Step 10 — Record the Final Result
paragraphs: Update STATE-001, DOC-STATE-001, DOC-INDEX-001, work-session history, milestone history, decision log, or other affected permanent records as required.

sections.title: Step 11 — Close the Session
paragraphs: Only after verification and documentation are complete should the session be committed, pushed, and closed.

sections.title: Required Work Session Record
paragraphs: A completed work-session record should preserve enough information to resume work without relying on memory.

rows: Clear description of the session goal.
What was true before the session began.
What the session was intended to accomplish.
Every important file, record, folder, workflow, or system changed.
Problems found and how they were corrected.
Approved choices and why they were made.
Reusable knowledge produced by the work.
Ideas preserved without interrupting the active path.

sections.title: Documentation Quality Rules
items: Do not guess or invent missing history.
Separate verified facts from recommendations and reconstructed history.
Preserve important reasoning, not just outcomes.
Use exact IDs, filenames, paths, versions, and test results when available.
Do not duplicate the same permanent truth across competing systems.
Keep enhancement ideas recorded but separate from current foundation work.
Write so another workstation or future session can continue without relying on one computer or one chat.

sections.title: Documentation Publication and Repository Verification
paragraphs: When a permanent Google document is required, use the controlled publishing process:
items: Verify the repository document.
Verify Studio Portal rendering.
Verify search and metadata.
If applicable, publish or update the approved human-readable document.
Record successful reconciliation before continuing.

sections.title: Relationship to Other Procedures
paragraphs: PROC-001 controls the work session. PROC-002 controls bug fixing when a failure interrupts the session. PROC-003 controls the documentation update that preserves the verified result.
items: PROC-001 — Work Session Procedure: start, execute, verify, document, and close focused work.
PROC-002 — Bug Fix Procedure: stop new work, identify the source and root cause, fix, and retest.
PROC-003 — Documentation Update Procedure: capture the verified knowledge produced by the work.

sections.title: Example — Completed Shared Search Work
paragraphs: A completed search-engine session should not be preserved only as 'search was fixed.' The documentation should capture the goal, the failed behavior, the wrong search.js location, the browser MIME type error, the corrected path, the successful console verification, the final search test, and the approved next action.

sections.title: Completion Checklist
items: ☐ Final work result was verified.
☐ Documentation-worthy knowledge was identified.
☐ Each item was classified into the correct record type.
☐ Reasoning was preserved where important.
☐ Structured records were updated.
☐ Records were validated.
☐ Required Google documents were populated and verified.
☐ Temporary files were removed after conversion.
☐ Current-state or documentation counts were updated where required.
☐ The exact next action was recorded.
☐ The session can be safely resumed from another location or workstation.

sections.title: Permanent Procedure Statement
paragraphs: Every completed Two Marshalls Studios work session must leave behind a verified, structured, and accessible record of what changed, why it changed, how it was tested, what was learned, and what happens next. Important studio knowledge must not remain trapped in chat history, temporary files, or one computer.
Two Marshalls Studios | TMS-OS Permanent Documentation

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified permanent document to the shared structured Document Template Engine.
Reconciled documentation update procedure with repository-first documentation governance.

verification.method: Reconciled against verified repository during WS086.
